summary: Typosquat of prettier ships install-time remote-code-execution dropper
details: pretie_x1 impersonates the legitimate `prettier` package (copying its description verbatim and reusing its keywords) and ships a malicious install hook. package.json declares `scripts.install: node cli.js`. cli.js exits early when `CI=true` or `npm_config_ignore_scripts=true` to evade automated scanners and CI pipelines, then invokes lib/mirror.js. lib/mirror.js holds two base64-encoded attacker URLs (decoding to https://deep-ai-guard.store/install_guard_d.js and https://hiring.aavcareer.ink/install_guard_d.js), downloads them to `os.tmpdir()` with TLS verification disabled (`rejectUnauthorized:false`), and spawns them via `process.execPath` in a detached, hidden process (`windowsHide`, `CREATE_NO_WINDOW`). Running `npm install pretie_x1` on a developer workstation results in execution of attacker-controlled JavaScript pulled from mutable third-party hosts.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
